title: Music for Gaming - From Dungeon Synth to Miles Davis
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=5sVEspUzpGY

Daniel (@dungeondive) delivers an epic exploration of music for tabletop gaming, splitting the discussion into passive listening (background atmosphere) and active listening (music demanding your full attention). For passive gaming soundscapes, he covers Tabletop Audio, Syrinscape, Table Tone, and the inescapable brilliance of Jeremy Soule's Elder Scrolls compositions via the Everest YouTube channel. Then there's dungeon synth—the chill-out room of black metal—where artists cloak themselves in robes and conjure atmosphere on vintage Casio keyboards. The Dungeon Synth Archives channel curates the genre if you're overwhelmed.

For cyberpunk vibes, Daniel points to vaporwave and mallsoft (Pad Chennington's '107 Vaporwave Albums You Should Know' is essential viewing). For horror, The Caretaker's plunderphonic ballroom decay is unmatched. But the video's second half pivots hard: Daniel's current obsession is Miles Davis's electric period (1969–1991), music so challenging it took him years to love. Bitches Brew, On the Corner, Big Fun, and the cyberpunk masterpiece Aura—this is active listening that grabs you by the collar and demands engagement.

----

What's your go-to album or playlist when the dice hit the table?
